title: Do schools kill creativity? | Sir Ken Robinson | TED

transcript.chinese: 早上好。
transcript.english: Good morning.

transcript.chinese: 你好吗？
transcript.english: How are you?

transcript.chinese: （观众）好。
transcript.english: (Audience) Good.

transcript.chinese: 太棒了，不是吗？
transcript.english: It's been great, hasn't it?

transcript.chinese: 我对整件事感到震惊。
transcript.english: I've been blown away by the whole thing.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，我要走了。
transcript.english: In fact, I'm leaving.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）整个会议贯穿了三个主题，这三个主题与我想讲的内容相关。
transcript.english: (Laughter) There have been three themes running through the conference, which are relevant to what I want to talk about.

transcript.chinese: 一是我们所做的所有演讲以及在座的所有人都证明了人类创造力的非凡证据；只是它的种类和范围。
transcript.english: One is the extraordinary evidence of human creativity in all of the presentations that we've had and in all of the people here; just the variety of it and the range of it.

transcript.chinese: 第二，它让我们处于一个不知道未来会发生什么的境地。
transcript.english: The second is that it's put us in a place where we have no idea what's going to happen in terms of the future.

transcript.chinese: 不知道这会如何发展。
transcript.english: No idea how this may play out.

transcript.chinese: 我对教育感兴趣。
transcript.english: I have an interest in education.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，我发现每个人都对教育感兴趣。
transcript.english: Actually, what I find is, everybody has an interest in education.

transcript.chinese: 不是吗？
transcript.english: Don't you?

transcript.chinese: 我觉得这很有趣。
transcript.english: I find this very interesting.

transcript.chinese: 如果你在一个晚宴上，你说你从事教育工作——坦率地说，实际上，你并不经常参加晚宴。
transcript.english: If you're at a dinner party, and you say you work in education -- actually, you're not often at dinner parties, frankly.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）如果你从事教育工作，就不会被问到。
transcript.english: (Laughter) If you work in education, you're not asked.

transcript.chinese: （笑声） 奇怪的是，你从来没有被问过。
transcript.english: (Laughter) And you're never asked back, curiously.

transcript.chinese: 这对我来说很奇怪。
transcript.english: That's strange to me.

transcript.chinese: 但如果你是，你对某人说，你知道，他们会说，“你是做什么的？”你说你从事教育工作，你可以看到他们脸上流着血。
transcript.english: But if you are, and you say to somebody, you know, they say, "What do you do?" and you say you work in education, you can see the blood run from their face.

transcript.chinese: 他们就像，“天啊。
transcript.english: They're like, "Oh my God.

transcript.chinese: 为什么是我？” （笑声） “我这一周都出去玩了一晚。” （笑声）但是如果你问起他们的教育，他们会把你钉在墙上，因为这是人们深入人心的事情之一，我说得对吗？
transcript.english: Why me?" (Laughter) "My one night out all week." (Laughter) But if you ask about their education, they pin you to the wall, because it's one of those things that goes deep with people, am I right?

transcript.chinese: 比如宗教、金钱和其他东西。
transcript.english: Like religion and money and other things.

transcript.chinese: 所以我对教育非常感兴趣，我想我们都是如此。
transcript.english: So I have a big interest in education, and I think we all do.

transcript.chinese: 我们对它有着巨大的既得利益，部分原因是教育旨在带我们进入我们无法掌握的未来。
transcript.english: We have a huge vested interest in it, partly because it's education that's meant to take us into this future that we can't grasp.

transcript.chinese: 如果你想一想，今年开始上学的孩子将在 2065 年退休。
transcript.english: If you think of it, children starting school this year will be retiring in 2065.

transcript.chinese: 尽管过去四天里大家都在展示各种专业知识，但没有人知道五年后的世界会是什么样子。
transcript.english: Nobody has a clue, despite all the expertise that's been on parade for the past four days, what the world will look like in five years' time.

transcript.chinese: 然而，我们应该为此教育他们。
transcript.english: And yet, we're meant to be educating them for it.

transcript.chinese: 所以我认为，不可预测性是非同寻常的。
transcript.english: So the unpredictability, I think, is extraordinary.

transcript.chinese: 第三部分是，尽管如此，我们都同意儿童拥有真正非凡的能力——他们的创新能力。
transcript.english: And the third part of this is that we've all agreed, nonetheless, on the really extraordinary capacities that children have -- their capacities for innovation.

transcript.chinese: 我是说，昨晚的塞丽娜真是个奇迹，不是吗？
transcript.english: I mean, Sirena last night was a marvel, wasn't she?

transcript.chinese: 只是看看她能做什么。
transcript.english: Just seeing what she could do.

transcript.chinese: 她很出色，但我认为她在整个童年时期并不出色。
transcript.english: And she's exceptional, but I think she's not, so to speak, exceptional in the whole of childhood.

transcript.chinese: 你所拥有的是一个具有非凡奉献精神并发现了才华的人。
transcript.english: What you have there is a person of extraordinary dedication who found a talent.

transcript.chinese: 我的观点是，所有的孩子都有巨大的才能，但我们却无情地浪费了他们。
transcript.english: And my contention is, all kids have tremendous talents, and we squander them, pretty ruthlessly.

transcript.chinese: 所以我想谈谈教育，我想谈谈创造力。
transcript.english: So I want to talk about education, and I want to talk about creativity.

transcript.chinese: 我的观点是，现在创造力在教育中与识字能力一样重要，我们应该同等对待它。
transcript.english: My contention is that creativity now is as important in education as literacy, and we should treat it with the same status.

transcript.chinese: （鼓掌）谢谢。
transcript.english: (Applause) Thank you.

transcript.chinese: （掌声）顺便说一句，就是这样。
transcript.english: (Applause) That was it, by the way.

transcript.chinese: 非常感谢。
transcript.english: Thank you very much.

transcript.chinese: （笑声） 那么，还剩 15 分钟。
transcript.english: (Laughter) So, 15 minutes left.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）“嗯，我出生了……”（笑声）我最近听到了一个很棒的故事——我喜欢讲它——讲的是一个正在上绘画课的小女孩。
transcript.english: (Laughter) "Well, I was born ... " (Laughter) I heard a great story recently -- I love telling it -- of a little girl who was in a drawing lesson.

transcript.chinese: 她六岁，在后面画画，老师说这个女孩几乎没有集中注意力，而在这堂绘画课上，她集中注意力了。
transcript.english: She was six, and she was at the back, drawing, and the teacher said this girl hardly ever paid attention, and in this drawing lesson, she did.

transcript.chinese: 老师很着迷。
transcript.english: The teacher was fascinated.

transcript.chinese: 她走到她身边，问道：“你在画什么？”女孩说：“我正在画上帝的画像。”
transcript.english: She went over to her, and she said, "What are you drawing?" And the girl said, "I'm drawing a picture of God."

transcript.chinese: 老师说：“但是没有人知道上帝长什么样。”女孩说：“一会儿就会的。” （笑声）
transcript.english: And the teacher said, "But nobody knows what God looks like." And the girl said, "They will in a minute." (Laughter)

transcript.chinese: 当我儿子在英国四岁的时候——说实话，实际上，他在任何地方都是四岁。
transcript.english: When my son was four in England -- actually, he was four everywhere, to be honest.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）如果我们严格来说，无论他去哪里，那一年他才四岁。
transcript.english: (Laughter) If we're being strict about it, wherever he went, he was four that year.

transcript.chinese: 他参加了耶稣诞生剧。
transcript.english: He was in the Nativity play.

transcript.chinese: 你还记得这个故事吗？
transcript.english: Do you remember the story?

transcript.chinese: （笑声）不，它很大，这是一个很大的故事。
transcript.english: (Laughter) No, it was big, it was a big story.

transcript.chinese: 梅尔·吉布森制作了续集，你可能已经看过了。
transcript.english: Mel Gibson did the sequel, you may have seen it.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）《耶稣诞生二》。但詹姆斯得到了约瑟夫的角色，这让我们很兴奋。
transcript.english: (Laughter) "Nativity II." But James got the part of Joseph, which we were thrilled about.

transcript.chinese: 我们认为这是主要部分之一。
transcript.english: We considered this to be one of the lead parts.

transcript.chinese: 我们的地方挤满了穿着 T 恤的特工：“詹姆斯·罗宾逊就是约瑟夫！” （笑声）他不必说话，但你知道三位国王是从哪里进来的吗？
transcript.english: We had the place crammed full of agents in T-shirts: "James Robinson IS Joseph!" (Laughter) He didn't have to speak, but you know the bit where the three kings come in?

transcript.chinese: 他们带着礼物而来：黄金、乳香和没药。
transcript.english: They come in bearing gifts, gold, frankincense and myrrh.

transcript.chinese: 这确实发生了。
transcript.english: This really happened.

transcript.chinese: 我们坐在那里，我认为他们只是打乱了顺序，因为我们后来和那个小男孩交谈并说：“你同意吗？”他们说：“是啊，为什么？
transcript.english: We were sitting there, and I think they just went out of sequence, because we talked to the little boy afterward and said, "You OK with that?" They said, "Yeah, why?

transcript.chinese: 那是错的吗？”他们刚刚换了。
transcript.english: Was that wrong?" They just switched.

transcript.chinese: 三个男孩进来了，都是四岁的孩子，头上顶着茶巾。
transcript.english: The three boys came in, four-year-olds with tea towels on their heads.

transcript.chinese: 他们把这些盒子放下，第一个男孩说：“我给你带来了金子。”第二个男孩说：“我给你带来没药。”第三个男孩说：“弗兰克寄来了这个。” （笑声）
transcript.english: They put these boxes down, and the first boy said, "I bring you gold." And the second boy said, "I bring you myrrh." And the third boy said, "Frank sent this." (Laughter)

transcript.chinese: 这些事情的共同点是孩子们会冒险。
transcript.english: What these things have in common is that kids will take a chance.

transcript.chinese: 如果他们不知道，他们就会尝试。
transcript.english: If they don't know, they'll have a go.

transcript.chinese: 我说得对吗？
transcript.english: Am I right?

transcript.chinese: 他们并不害怕犯错。
transcript.english: They're not frightened of being wrong.

transcript.chinese: 我并不是说犯错和有创造力是一回事。
transcript.english: I don't mean to say that being wrong is the same thing as being creative.

transcript.chinese: 我们所知道的是，如果你不准备犯错，你就永远不会想出任何原创的东西——如果你不准备犯错。
transcript.english: What we do know is, if you're not prepared to be wrong, you'll never come up with anything original -- if you're not prepared to be wrong.

transcript.chinese: 当他们成年时，大多数孩子已经失去了这种能力。
transcript.english: And by the time they get to be adults, most kids have lost that capacity.

transcript.chinese: 他们变得害怕犯错。
transcript.english: They have become frightened of being wrong.

transcript.chinese: 我们就是这样经营我们的公司的。
transcript.english: And we run our companies like this.

transcript.chinese: 我们污蔑错误。
transcript.english: We stigmatize mistakes.

transcript.chinese: 我们现在运行的国家教育系统中，错误是最糟糕的事情。
transcript.english: And we're now running national education systems where mistakes are the worst thing you can make.

transcript.chinese: 结果是我们正在教育人们发挥他们的创造力。
transcript.english: And the result is that we are educating people out of their creative capacities.

transcript.chinese: 毕加索曾经说过这样的话，他说所有的孩子都是天生的艺术家。
transcript.english: Picasso once said this, he said that all children are born artists.

transcript.chinese: 问题是随着我们的成长，我们仍然是一名艺术家。
transcript.english: The problem is to remain an artist as we grow up.

transcript.chinese: 我坚信这一点：我们不是从创造力中成长起来的，而是从创造力中成长出来的。
transcript.english: I believe this passionately, that we don't grow into creativity, we grow out of it.

transcript.chinese: 或者更确切地说，我们从中得到了教育。
transcript.english: Or rather, we get educated out of it.

transcript.chinese: 那么这是为什么呢？
transcript.english: So why is this?

transcript.chinese: 大约五年前，我一直住在埃文河畔斯特拉特福。
transcript.english: I lived in Stratford-on-Avon until about five years ago.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，我们从斯特拉特福搬到了洛杉矶。
transcript.english: In fact, we moved from Stratford to Los Angeles.

transcript.chinese: 所以你可以想象这是一个多么无缝的过渡。
transcript.english: So you can imagine what a seamless transition this was.

transcript.chinese: （笑声） 事实上，我们住在斯特拉特福郊外一个叫斯尼特菲尔德的地方，那是莎士比亚父亲的出生地。
transcript.english: (Laughter) Actually, we lived in a place called Snitterfield, just outside Stratford, which is where Shakespeare's father was born.

transcript.chinese: 您有新的想法吗？
transcript.english: Are you struck by a new thought?

transcript.chinese: 我是。
transcript.english: I was.

transcript.chinese: 你不认为莎士比亚有父亲，是吗？
transcript.english: You don't think of Shakespeare having a father, do you?

transcript.chinese: 你？
transcript.english: Do you?

transcript.chinese: 因为你不认为莎士比亚是个孩子，是吗？
transcript.english: Because you don't think of Shakespeare being a child, do you?

transcript.chinese: 莎士比亚七岁？
transcript.english: Shakespeare being seven?

transcript.chinese: 我从来没有想过。
transcript.english: I never thought of it.

transcript.chinese: 我的意思是，他在某个时候七岁。
transcript.english: I mean, he was seven at some point.

transcript.chinese: 他在某人的英语课上，不是吗？
transcript.english: He was in somebody's English class, wasn't he?

transcript.chinese: （笑声） 那会有多烦人呢？
transcript.english: (Laughter) How annoying would that be?

transcript.chinese: （笑声）“必须更加努力。” （笑声） 被他爸爸送去睡觉，对莎士比亚说：“现在就去睡觉！”献给威廉·莎士比亚。
transcript.english: (Laughter) "Must try harder." (Laughter) Being sent to bed by his dad, to Shakespeare, "Go to bed, now!" To William Shakespeare.

transcript.chinese: “把铅笔放下！” （笑声）“别再这样说话了。” （笑声）“这让每个人都感到困惑。” （笑声） 不管怎样，我们从斯特拉特福搬到了洛杉矶，我只想说一下关于转变的事情。
transcript.english: "And put the pencil down!" (Laughter) "And stop speaking like that." (Laughter) "It's confusing everybody." (Laughter) Anyway, we moved from Stratford to Los Angeles, and I just want to say a word about the transition.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，我儿子不想来。
transcript.english: Actually, my son didn't want to come.

transcript.chinese: 我有两个孩子；他现在 21 岁，我女儿 16 岁。
transcript.english: I've got two kids; he's 21 now, my daughter's 16.

transcript.chinese: 他不想来洛杉矶。
transcript.english: He didn't want to come to Los Angeles.

transcript.chinese: 他很喜欢，但他在英国有一个女朋友。
transcript.english: He loved it, but he had a girlfriend in England.

transcript.chinese: 这是他一生的挚爱，莎拉。
transcript.english: This was the love of his life, Sarah.

transcript.chinese: 他认识她一个月了。
transcript.english: He'd known her for a month.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）请注意，他们已经过了四周年纪念日，因为对于你 16 岁来说这是一段很长的时间。
transcript.english: (Laughter) Mind you, they'd had their fourth anniversary, because it's a long time when you're 16.

transcript.chinese: 他在飞机上真的很沮丧。
transcript.english: He was really upset on the plane.

transcript.chinese: 他说：“我再也找不到像莎拉这样的女孩了。”坦率地说，我们对此感到非常高兴——（笑声）
transcript.english: He said, "I'll never find another girl like Sarah." And we were rather pleased about that, frankly -- (Laughter)

transcript.chinese: 因为她是我们离开这个国家的主要原因。
transcript.english: because she was the main reason we were leaving the country.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）但是，当你搬到美国并环游世界时，有件事会让你震惊：地球上的每个教育系统都有相同的科目等级。
transcript.english: (Laughter) But something strikes you when you move to America and travel around the world: every education system on earth has the same hierarchy of subjects.

transcript.chinese: 每个人。
transcript.english: Every one.

transcript.chinese: 你去哪里并不重要。
transcript.english: Doesn't matter where you go.

transcript.chinese: 你可能会认为情况会是另外一种情况，但事实并非如此。
transcript.english: You'd think it would be otherwise, but it isn't.

transcript.chinese: 最上面的是数学和语言，然后是人文学科。
transcript.english: At the top are mathematics and languages, then the humanities.

transcript.chinese: 最底层的是艺术。
transcript.english: At the bottom are the arts.

transcript.chinese: 地球上到处都是。
transcript.english: Everywhere on earth.

transcript.chinese: 几乎在每个系统中，艺术内部也存在等级制度。
transcript.english: And in pretty much every system, too, there's a hierarchy within the arts.

transcript.chinese: 艺术和音乐在学校中的地位通常高于戏剧和舞蹈。
transcript.english: Art and music are normally given a higher status in schools than drama and dance.

transcript.chinese: 世界上没有一个教育系统能够像教数学一样每天教孩子们舞蹈。
transcript.english: There isn't an education system on the planet that teaches dance every day to children the way we teach them mathematics.

transcript.chinese: 为什么？
transcript.english: Why?

transcript.chinese: 为什么不呢？
transcript.english: Why not?

transcript.chinese: 我觉得这个还是比较重要的。
transcript.english: I think this is rather important.

transcript.chinese: 我认为数学很重要，但舞蹈也很重要。
transcript.english: I think math is very important, but so is dance.

transcript.chinese: 如果允许的话，孩子们总是会跳舞，我们都这样做。
transcript.english: Children dance all the time if they're allowed to, we all do.

transcript.chinese: 我们都有身体，不是吗？
transcript.english: We all have bodies, don't we?

transcript.chinese: 我错过了一个会议吗？
transcript.english: Did I miss a meeting?

transcript.chinese: （笑声）说实话，随着孩子的成长，我们开始从腰部以上逐步教育他们。
transcript.english: (Laughter) Truthfully, what happens is, as children grow up, we start to educate them progressively from the waist up.

transcript.chinese: 然后我们把注意力集中在他们的头上。
transcript.english: And then we focus on their heads.

transcript.chinese: 并且稍微偏向一侧。
transcript.english: And slightly to one side.

transcript.chinese: 如果你以外星人的身份参观教育并说“公共教育是为了什么？”我想你必须得出结论，如果你看看结果，谁真正成功了，谁做了他们应该做的一切，谁得到了所有的布朗尼点，谁是赢家——我认为你必须得出结论公共教育的全部目的
transcript.english: If you were to visit education as an alien and say "What's it for, public education?" I think you'd have to conclude, if you look at the output, who really succeeds by this, who does everything they should, who gets all the brownie points, who are the winners -- I think you'd have to conclude the whole purpose of public education

transcript.chinese: 全世界都在培养大学教授。
transcript.english: throughout the world is to produce university professors.

transcript.chinese: 不是吗？
transcript.english: Isn't it?

transcript.chinese: 他们是那些脱颖而出的人。
transcript.english: They're the people who come out the top.

transcript.chinese: 我曾经也是其中之一，所以就在那里。
transcript.english: And I used to be one, so there.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）我喜欢大学教授，但是，你知道，我们不应该把他们视为所有人类成就的最高点。
transcript.english: (Laughter) And I like university professors, but, you know, we shouldn't hold them up as the high-water mark of all human achievement.

transcript.chinese: 它们只是生命的一种形式。
transcript.english: They're just a form of life.

transcript.chinese: 生命的另一种形式。
transcript.english: Another form of life.

transcript.chinese: 但他们很好奇。
transcript.english: But they're rather curious.

transcript.chinese: 我这样说是出于对他们的喜爱：教授们有些好奇。
transcript.english: And I say this out of affection for them: there's something curious about professors.

transcript.chinese: 根据我的经验——不是全部，但通常是——他们生活在自己的头脑中。
transcript.english: In my experience -- not all of them, but typically -- they live in their heads.

transcript.chinese: 他们住在那儿，稍微偏向一侧。
transcript.english: They live up there and slightly to one side.

transcript.chinese: 你知道，他们在某种程度上是脱离实体的。
transcript.english: They're disembodied, you know, in a kind of literal way.

transcript.chinese: 他们将身体视为头部的一种交通工具。
transcript.english: They look upon their body as a form of transport for their heads.

transcript.chinese: （笑声） 不是吗？
transcript.english: (Laughter) Don't they?

transcript.chinese: 这是让他们集中精力参加会议的一种方式。
transcript.english: It's a way of getting their head to meetings.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）如果你想要灵魂出窍经历的真实证据，顺便说一句，你可以去参加一个高级学者的住宿会议，然后在最后一晚去迪斯科舞厅。
transcript.english: (Laughter) If you want real evidence of out-of-body experiences, by the way, get yourself along to a residential conference of senior academics and pop into the discotheque on the final night.

transcript.chinese: （笑声） 在那里，你会看到它。
transcript.english: (Laughter) And there, you will see it.

transcript.chinese: 成年男女无法控制地扭动，失去节拍。
transcript.english: Grown men and women writhing uncontrollably, off the beat.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）等到它结束，这样他们就可以回家写一篇关于它的论文。
transcript.english: (Laughter) Waiting until it ends, so they can go home and write a paper about it.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）我们的教育体系是以学术能力为基础的。
transcript.english: (Laughter) Our education system is predicated on the idea of academic ability.

transcript.chinese: 这是有原因的。
transcript.english: And there's a reason.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，在 19 世纪之前，世界各地都没有公共教育系统。
transcript.english: Around the world, there were no public systems of education, really, before the 19th century.

transcript.chinese: 它们都是为了适应工业化的需要而产生的。
transcript.english: They all came into being to meet the needs of industrialism.

transcript.chinese: 因此，层次结构植根于两个想法。
transcript.english: So the hierarchy is rooted on two ideas.

transcript.chinese: 第一，对工作最有用的主题位于顶部。
transcript.english: Number one, that the most useful subjects for work are at the top.

transcript.chinese: 所以当你还是个孩子的时候，你可能会被善意地引导远离学校里的东西，远离你喜欢的东西，因为你永远找不到这样做的工作。
transcript.english: So you were probably steered benignly away from things at school when you were a kid, things you liked, on the grounds you would never get a job doing that.

transcript.chinese: 是这样吗？
transcript.english: Is that right?

transcript.chinese: “不做音乐，你不会成为音乐家；不做艺术，你不会成为艺术家。”善意的建议——现在看来是大错特错了。
transcript.english: "Don't do music, you're not going to be a musician; don't do art, you won't be an artist." Benign advice -- now, profoundly mistaken.

transcript.chinese: 整个世界都陷入了一场革命。
transcript.english: The whole world is engulfed in a revolution.

transcript.chinese: 第二个是学术能力，它确实主导了我们对智力的看法，因为大学按照自己的形象设计了系统。
transcript.english: And the second is academic ability, which has really come to dominate our view of intelligence, because the universities design the system in their image.

transcript.chinese: 如果你想一想，世界各地的整个公共教育体系都是一个漫长的大学入学过程。
transcript.english: If you think of it, the whole system of public education around the world is a protracted process of university entrance.

transcript.chinese: 结果是，许多才华横溢、才华横溢、富有创造力的人认为自己不是，因为他们在学校擅长的事情没有得到重视，或者实际上受到了侮辱。
transcript.english: And the consequence is that many highly talented, brilliant, creative people think they're not, because the thing they were good at at school wasn't valued, or was actually stigmatized.

transcript.chinese: 我认为我们不能再这样下去了。
transcript.english: And I think we can't afford to go on that way.

transcript.chinese: 据联合国教科文组织称，未来 30 年，全世界通过教育毕业的人数将比历史开始以来还要多。
transcript.english: In the next 30 years, according to UNESCO, more people worldwide will be graduating through education than since the beginning of history.

transcript.chinese: 更多人。
transcript.english: More people.

transcript.chinese: 这是我们讨论过的所有事情的结合：技术及其对工作的变革性影响，以及人口统计和人口的巨大爆炸。
transcript.english: And it's the combination of all the things we've talked about: technology and its transformational effect on work, and demography and the huge explosion in population.

transcript.chinese: 突然间，学位变得毫无价值。
transcript.english: Suddenly, degrees aren't worth anything.

transcript.chinese: 这不是真的吗？
transcript.english: Isn't that true?

transcript.chinese: 当我还是一名学生时，如果你有学位，你就有工作。
transcript.english: When I was a student, if you had a degree, you had a job.

transcript.chinese: 如果你没有工作，那是因为你不想要一份工作。
transcript.english: If you didn't have a job, it's because you didn't want one.

transcript.chinese: 坦白说，我并不想要一个。
transcript.english: And I didn't want one, frankly.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）但是现在有学位的孩子经常回家继续玩电子游戏，因为你需要一个硕士学位，而以前的工作需要学士学位，而现在你需要另一个博士学位。
transcript.english: (Laughter) But now kids with degrees are often heading home to carry on playing video games, because you need an MA where the previous job required a BA, and now you need a PhD for the other.

transcript.chinese: 这是一个学术膨胀的过程。
transcript.english: It's a process of academic inflation.

transcript.chinese: 这表明整个教育结构正在我们脚下发生变化。
transcript.english: And it indicates the whole structure of education is shifting beneath our feet.

transcript.chinese: 我们需要从根本上重新思考我们对情报的看法。
transcript.english: We need to radically rethink our view of intelligence.

transcript.chinese: 我们知道关于智力的三件事。
transcript.english: We know three things about intelligence.

transcript.chinese: 一、它是多样化的。
transcript.english: One, it's diverse.

transcript.chinese: 我们以我们体验世界的各种方式思考这个世界。
transcript.english: We think about the world in all the ways that we experience it.

transcript.chinese: 我们用视觉思考、用声音思考、用动觉思考。
transcript.english: We think visually, we think in sound, we think kinesthetically.

transcript.chinese: 我们以抽象的方式思考，我们以运动的方式思考。
transcript.english: We think in abstract terms, we think in movement.

transcript.chinese: 其次，智力是动态的。
transcript.english: Secondly, intelligence is dynamic.

transcript.chinese: 如果你观察一下人脑的相互作用，正如我们昨天从许多演讲中听到的那样，智能是具有奇妙的交互性的。
transcript.english: If you look at the interactions of a human brain, as we heard yesterday from a number of presentations, intelligence is wonderfully interactive.

transcript.chinese: 大脑不分为区室。
transcript.english: The brain isn't divided into compartments.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，创造力——我将其定义为产生有价值的原创想法的过程——通常是通过不同学科看待事物的方式的相互作用而产生的。
transcript.english: In fact, creativity -- which I define as the process of having original ideas that have value -- more often than not comes about through the interaction of different disciplinary ways of seeing things.

transcript.chinese: 顺便说一下，有一条神经连接大脑的两半，称为胼胝体。
transcript.english: By the way, there's a shaft of nerves that joins the two halves of the brain, called the corpus callosum.

transcript.chinese: 女性则较厚。
transcript.english: It's thicker in women.

transcript.chinese: 继昨天的海伦之后，这可能就是女性更擅长同时处理多项任务的原因。
transcript.english: Following off from Helen yesterday, this is probably why women are better at multitasking.

transcript.chinese: 因为你就是，不是吗？
transcript.english: Because you are, aren't you?

transcript.chinese: 有大量的研究，但我从我的个人生活中知道了这一点。
transcript.english: There's a raft of research, but I know it from my personal life.

transcript.chinese: 如果我的妻子在家做饭，这并不常见......
transcript.english: If my wife is cooking a meal at home, which is not often ...

transcript.chinese: 谢天谢地。
transcript.english: thankfully.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）不，她在某些事情上很擅长。
transcript.english: (Laughter) No, she's good at some things.

transcript.chinese: 但如果她正在做饭，她正在打电话与人打交道，她正在与孩子们交谈，她正在粉刷天花板 - （笑声）她正在这里进行心脏直视手术。
transcript.english: But if she's cooking, she's dealing with people on the phone, she's talking to the kids, she's painting the ceiling -- (Laughter) she's doing open-heart surgery over here.

transcript.chinese: 如果我正在做饭，门就关上了，孩子们出去了，电话挂断了，如果她进来，我就会生气。
transcript.english: If I'm cooking, the door is shut, the kids are out, the phone's on the hook, if she comes in, I get annoyed.

transcript.chinese: 我说：“特里，拜托，我想在这里煎一个鸡蛋。” （笑声）“让我休息一下。” （笑声）事实上，你知道那句古老的哲学道理吗？“如果一棵树倒在森林里，而没有人听到，那么它就发生了吗？”还记得那个老栗子吗？
transcript.english: I say, "Terry, please, I'm trying to fry an egg in here." (Laughter) "Give me a break." (Laughter) Actually, do you know that old philosophical thing, "If a tree falls in a forest, and nobody hears it, did it happen?" Remember that old chestnut?

transcript.chinese: 我最近看到一件很棒的T恤，上面写着：“如果一个男人在森林里说出自己的想法，而没有女人听到他的声音，那么他还是错了吗？” （笑声）
transcript.english: I saw a great T-shirt recently, which said, "If a man speaks his mind in a forest, and no woman hears him, is he still wrong?" (Laughter)

transcript.chinese: 关于智力的第三点是，它是独特的。
transcript.english: And the third thing about intelligence is, it's distinct.

transcript.chinese: 我目前正在写一本名为《顿悟》的新书，该书基于对人们如何发现自己的才能的一系列采访。
transcript.english: I'm doing a new book at the moment called "Epiphany," which is based on a series of interviews with people about how they discovered their talent.

transcript.chinese: 我对人们如何到达那里感到着迷。
transcript.english: I'm fascinated by how people got to be there.

transcript.chinese: 这实际上是由我与一位可能大多数人从未听说过的出色女性吉莉安·林恩 (Gillian Lynne) 的一次对话引发的。
transcript.english: It's really prompted by a conversation I had with a wonderful woman who maybe most people have never heard of, Gillian Lynne.

transcript.chinese: 你听说过她吗？
transcript.english: Have you heard of her?

transcript.chinese: 有些有。
transcript.english: Some have.

transcript.chinese: 她是一名编舞家，每个人都知道她的工作。
transcript.english: She's a choreographer, and everybody knows her work.

transcript.chinese: 她出演了《猫》和《歌剧魅影》。她很棒。
transcript.english: She did "Cats" and "Phantom of the Opera." She's wonderful.

transcript.chinese: 如你所见，我曾经是皇家芭蕾舞团的董事会成员。
transcript.english: I used to be on the board of The Royal Ballet, as you can see.

transcript.chinese: （笑声） 有一天，阿娇和我共进午餐。
transcript.english: (Laughter) Gillian and I had lunch one day.

transcript.chinese: 我说：“你是怎么成为一名舞者的？”这很有趣。
transcript.english: I said, "How did you get to be a dancer?" It was interesting.

transcript.chinese: 当她在学校的时候，她真的很绝望。
transcript.english: When she was at school, she was really hopeless.

transcript.chinese: 三十年代，学校写信给她的父母说：“我们认为阿娇有学习障碍。”她无法集中注意力；她坐立不安。
transcript.english: And the school, in the '30s, wrote to her parents and said, "We think Gillian has a learning disorder." She couldn't concentrate; she was fidgeting.

transcript.chinese: 我想现在他们会说她患有多动症。
transcript.english: I think now they'd say she had ADHD.

transcript.chinese: 你不会吗？
transcript.english: Wouldn't you?

transcript.chinese: 但那是 20 世纪 30 年代，此时 ADHD 还没有被发明。
transcript.english: But this was the 1930s, and ADHD hadn't been invented at this point.

transcript.chinese: 这不是一个可用的条件。
transcript.english: It wasn't an available condition.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）人们并不知道他们可以拥有它。
transcript.english: (Laughter) People weren't aware they could have that.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）不管怎样，她还是去看了这位专家。
transcript.english: (Laughter) Anyway, she went to see this specialist.

transcript.chinese: 所以，在这个橡木镶板的房间里，她和她的母亲一起在那里，她被引导坐在最后的这张椅子上，她双手坐了20分钟，而这个男人和她母亲谈论阿娇在学校遇到的所有问题，因为她打扰别人，她的作业总是迟到，等等。
transcript.english: So, this oak-paneled room, and she was there with her mother, and she was led and sat on this chair at the end, and she sat on her hands for 20 minutes, while this man talked to her mother about all the problems Gillian was having at school, because she was disturbing people, her homework was always late, and so on.

transcript.chinese: 八岁的小孩子。
transcript.english: Little kid of eight.

transcript.chinese: 最后，医生走到阿娇身边坐下，说道：“你妈妈告诉我的这些事我都听了。
transcript.english: In the end, the doctor went and sat next to Gillian and said, "I've listened to all these things your mother's told me.

transcript.chinese: 我需要和她私下谈谈。
transcript.english: I need to speak to her privately.

transcript.chinese: 在这里等一下。
transcript.english: Wait here.

transcript.chinese: 我们会回来的。
transcript.english: We'll be back.

transcript.chinese: 我们不会太久。”然后他们就离开了她。
transcript.english: We won't be very long," and they went and left her.

transcript.chinese: 但当他们走出房间时，他打开了办公桌上的收音机。
transcript.english: But as they went out of the room, he turned on the radio that was sitting on his desk.

transcript.chinese: 当他们走出房间时，他对她母亲说：“站着看着她。”他们一离开房间，她就站了起来，随着音乐移动。
transcript.english: And when they got out of the room, he said to her mother, "Just stand and watch her." And the minute they left the room, she was on her feet, moving to the music.

transcript.chinese: 他们看了几分钟，然后他转向她的母亲说：“林恩夫人，吉莉安没有生病。
transcript.english: And they watched for a few minutes, and he turned to her mother and said, "Mrs.

transcript.chinese: 她是一名舞蹈演员。
transcript.english: Lynne, Gillian isn't sick.

transcript.chinese: 带她去舞蹈学校。”我说：“发生什么事了？”她说：“她做到了。
transcript.english: She's a dancer.

transcript.chinese: 我无法告诉你那有多美妙。
transcript.english: Take her to a dance school." I said, "What happened?" She said, "She did.

transcript.chinese: 我们走进这个房间，里面挤满了像我这样的人——那些无法静坐的人，那些必须动起来思考的人。”谁必须动起来思考。
transcript.english: I can't tell you how wonderful it was.

transcript.chinese: 他们跳芭蕾、踢踏舞、爵士乐；他们做的是现代的；他们做了当代的。
transcript.english: We walked in this room, and it was full of people like me -- people who couldn't sit still, people who had to move to think." Who had to move to think.

transcript.chinese: 她最终参加了皇家芭蕾舞学校的试镜。
transcript.english: They did ballet, they did tap, jazz; they did modern; they did contemporary.

transcript.chinese: 她成为一名独奏家；她在皇家芭蕾舞团有着辉煌的职业生涯。
transcript.english: She was eventually auditioned for the Royal Ballet School.

transcript.chinese: 她最终从皇家芭蕾舞学校毕业，创立了吉莉安·林恩舞蹈团，结识了安德鲁·劳埃德·韦伯。
transcript.english: She became a soloist; she had a wonderful career at the Royal Ballet.

transcript.chinese: 她创作了一些历史上最成功的音乐剧作品，为数百万人带来了欢乐，而且她还是一位千万富翁。
transcript.english: She eventually graduated from the Royal Ballet School, founded the Gillian Lynne Dance Company, met Andrew Lloyd Webber.

transcript.chinese: 其他人可能会让她服用药物并告诉她冷静下来。
transcript.english: She's been responsible for some of the most successful musical theater productions in history, she's given pleasure to millions, and she's a multimillionaire.

transcript.chinese: （掌声）我认为事情是这样的：前几天晚上阿尔·戈尔谈到了生态学和雷切尔·卡森引发的革命。
transcript.english: Somebody else might have put her on medication and told her to calm down.

transcript.chinese: 我相信，我们未来唯一的希望是采用一种新的人类生态学概念，开始重建我们对人类能力丰富性的概念。
transcript.english: (Applause) What I think it comes to is this: Al Gore spoke the other night about ecology and the revolution that was triggered by Rachel Carson.

transcript.chinese: 我们的教育体系挖掘了我们的思想，就像我们在地球上开采某种特定商品一样。
transcript.english: I believe our only hope for the future is to adopt a new conception of human ecology, one in which we start to reconstitute our conception of the richness of human capacity.

transcript.chinese: 对于未来，它不会为我们服务。
transcript.english: Our education system has mined our minds in the way that we strip-mine the earth for a particular commodity.

transcript.chinese: 我们必须重新思考我们教育孩子的基本原则。
transcript.english: And for the future, it won't serve us.

transcript.chinese: 乔纳斯·索尔克（Jonas Salk）有一句精彩的话，他说：“如果所有的昆虫都从地球上消失，50年内，地球上的所有生命都将终结。
transcript.english: We have to rethink the fundamental principles on which we're educating our children.

transcript.chinese: 乔纳斯·索尔克（Jonas Salk）有一句精彩的话，他说：“如果所有的昆虫都从地球上消失，50年内，地球上的所有生命都将终结。
transcript.english: There was a wonderful quote by Jonas Salk, who said, "If all the insects were to disappear from the Earth, within 50 years, all life on Earth would end.

transcript.chinese: 如果所有人类在 50 年内从地球上消失，所有形式的生命都会蓬勃发展。”他是对的。
transcript.english: If all human beings disappeared from the Earth, within 50 years, all forms of life would flourish." And he's right.

transcript.chinese: TED 所庆祝的是人类想象力的礼物。
transcript.english: What TED celebrates is the gift of the human imagination.

transcript.chinese: 我们现在必须小心，明智地使用这份礼物，避免我们谈论过的一些情况。
transcript.english: We have to be careful now that we use this gift wisely, and that we avert some of the scenarios that we've talked about.

transcript.chinese: 而我们要做到这一点的唯一方法就是看到我们的创造力的丰富性，并看到我们的孩子的希望。
transcript.english: And the only way we'll do it is by seeing our creative capacities for the richness they are and seeing our children for the hope that they are.

transcript.chinese: 我们的任务是教育他们的整体，让他们能够面对这个未来——我们可能看不到这个未来，但我们的工作是帮助他们有所作为
transcript.english: And our task is to educate their whole being, so they can face this future.

transcript.chinese: （掌声）。
transcript.english: By the way -- we may not see this future, but they will.

transcript.chinese: 我们的工作就是帮助他们有所作为。
transcript.english: And our job is to help them make something of it.

transcript.chinese: 非常感谢。
transcript.english: Thank you very much.

transcript.chinese: （掌声）
transcript.english: (Applause)
